EF1 TORNADO BEGAN IN A WOODED AREA BETWEEN RIVER RD AND GW SMITH RD. SNAPPED AND UPROOTED DOZENS OF TREES ALONG GW SMITH RD. A FEW HAY BARNS WERE DAMAGED. *** 19 INJ *** THIS TORNADO STARTED ON THE WEST SIDE OF OAK GROVE...LOW END EF4 DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR THE HIGH SCHOOL AND TO A HOUSING AREA JUST TO THE SW OF THAT LOCATION WHERE A WELL BUILD BRICK HOME WAS LEVLED. VEHILCES WERE ALSO THROWN A CONSIDERABLE DISTANCE. EF3 DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG MUCH OF THE PATH THROUGH WEST HATTIESBURG AND IN THE CITY OF HATTIESBURG. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED JUST INSIDE NW PERRY COUNTY. ESTIMATED PATH LENGTH WAS 21 MI.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90MPH...MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 75YDS AND PATH LENGTH OF 2.5 MILES. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 65 WHERE A ROOF WAS TORN OFF A MOBILE HOME AND AN 18 WHEELER REAR TRAILER WAS TURNED OVER. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST AND CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE TO A BRICK HOUSE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO TRAXLER ROAD WHERE ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A HOME. A SHED WAS DESTROYED AND ANOTHER SHED WAS FLIPPED. A TRUCK AND TRAILER WAS PUSHED AROUND 15 FEET.

THE TORNADO STARTED ALONG SMITH LANE AND MOVED NE...THE TORNADO PEAKED AS IT MOVED ALONG GREEN CREEK RD AND CARSON-BUNKER HILL RD. TWO WELL BUILT FRAME HOMES WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED...POWER POLES SNAPPED AND A WELL BUILT FARM BUILDING WAS DESTROYED. SEVERAL OTHER HOMES AND BUILDINGS SUSTAINED MINOR OR MODERATE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED BEFORE IT REACHED HWY 42 WEST OF BASSFIELD. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. PATH LENGHT WAS 6.3 MI...400 YDS WIDE.
